title: Tunable Odd-Parity Spin Splittings in Altermagnets
abstract: Momentum-dependent spin splitting and its relation to inversion ($P$) and time-reversal ($T$) symmetries are central to nonrelativistic spintronics. Representative examples include collinear altermagnets with $(P,T)=(+,-)$ and non-collinear odd-parity magnets with $(P,T)=(-,+)$. In this work, we develop a theoretical framework to induce odd-parity spin splittings in the more abundant collinear altermagnets through two mechanisms: driving by a two-color linearly polarized light field or coupling to a $P$-odd loop-current order. Properly phase-locked two-color driving induces a static $(P,T)=(-,-)$ order, symmetry-equivalent to a translationally invariant $P$-odd loop-current order. Coupling this order to an altermagnet produces a controllable mixed-parity spin texture, opening new avenues for the electrical and optical manipulation of spin-polarized currents in spintronics applications. The same mechanism applied to a collinear $PT$-symmetric magnet induces a distinct $(P,T)=(+,+)$ state with a nonrelativistic dissipationless anomalous spin Hall conductivity. We present group-theory and microscopic Floquet theory to highlight the emergent responses.
summary: This paper proposes a method to engineer new spin-splitting symmetries in altermagnets using light or loop-current orders. By inducing odd-parity spin textures, it enables new ways to manipulate spin-polarized currents for spintronic applications. The work also predicts a dissipationless spin Hall effect in driven PT-symmetric magnets.
main_problem: Developing a theoretical framework to induce tunable odd-parity spin splittings in collinear altermagnets using two-color light driving or coupling to loop-current orders.
main_result: The study demonstrates that two-color light driving or P-odd loop-current orders can create mixed-parity spin textures in altermagnets and induce a nonrelativistic dissipationless anomalous spin Hall conductivity in PT-symmetric magnets.
method: The work employs group-theoretical analysis, Landau theory, and microscopic Floquet theory (using the high-frequency and weak-intensity limits) alongside the Kubo formula for transport properties.
model_or_system: The study focuses on collinear altermagnets (P,T = +, -) and PT-symmetric magnets (P,T = -, -) on square (D4h) and honeycomb (D6h) lattices, using a two-sublattice Hamiltonian with k-dependent hopping and exchange interactions.
key_observables: Momentum-dependent spin splitting (even and odd parity), spin-polarized currents, and dissipationless anomalous spin Hall conductivity.
important_parameters: Two-color light driving with a 2:1 frequency ratio, relative phase between light components, high-frequency limit (omega >> bandwidth), and weak light intensity.
main_assumptions: Assumes the high-frequency limit, small driving amplitude (A << 1), and collinear spin ordering along the z-axis.
figures_summary: Figures show Fermi surface evolution (undriven vs. driven), the honeycomb lattice structure for PT-symmetric magnets, and different types of loop-current orders in square lattices.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of spin-splitting symmetry, develops a theoretical framework using group theory and Floquet theory, analyzes the response of altermagnets and PT-symmetric magnets to light and loop-currents, and concludes with transport property calculations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Four classes of magnetic states classified by (P, T) symmetry and the transitions between them. Two-color lin- early polarized light and P-odd loop-current order both carry (P, T) = (−, −) symmetry. When coupled to altermagnets, they induce nonrelativistic odd-parity spin splitting; when coupled to collinear PT-symmetric magnets, they generate dissipationless anomalous spin Hall conductivity.

FIG. 2. Spin-split Fermi surfaces. (Left panel) In the ab- sence of light, the altermagnet exhibits even-parity kxkyσz spin splitting. (Right panel) Under two-color driving, an ad- ditional odd-parity kyσz spin splitting is induced. Parameters (in eV): t0 = −0.02, tx0 = 0.33, tz0 = 0.03, µ = −0.14, and J = 0.05, corresponding to the kz = 0 plane of MnF2[48]. The light parameters are ω = 0.12 eV and √

FIG. 3. Light-induced nonrelativistic dissipationless anoma- lous spin Hall conductivities σl ij for a collinear PT-symmetric magnet with spins aligned along ±ˆz. The longitudinal spin conductivities σl xx and σl yy vanish. Parameters (in eV): t0 = 0.05, t1 = 0.2, J = 0.03, µ = 0.2, and ω = 0.12 eV, with lattice constant a0 = 5 ˚A.

FIG. 4. The honeycomb lattice for a PT-symmetric magnet. Spin orderings are opposite on the two sublattices (white and black sites). The P-odd loop-current order is indicated by col- ored arrows within the bottom unit cell and is identical in ev- ery unit cell. Solid and dashed arrows denote inter-sublattice and intra-sublattice currents, respectively.

FIG. 5. Translationally invariant P-odd loop-current order on bipartite square lattice. Inversion symmetry is on-site. (Left) loop-current with inter-sublattice current and net intra-sublattice current. (Right) loop-current with opposite intra-sublattice current on the two sublattices.

Spintronics.note: The paper provides a direct extension to spintronics by demonstrating how two-color light driving can engineer new spin-polarized currents and anomalous spin Hall conductivity in magnetic systems.
